tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

title.description: Title of the memory to delete. Must match exactly.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>
Remember that my favorite programming language is TypeScript.

response.delta: a memory about their favorite programming language.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  
</blueprint>
I'm going to LA next week. Find me some good hotels and remember the recommendations.

content.text: The user wants hotel recommendations in LA and wants me to save them. Let me search for good hotels in LA and also check if I have any existing memories about their preferences.
I'll search for great hotels in LA and check if I have any existing preferences saved for you!

content.text:
Here are some excellent LA hotel recommendations across different categories and budgets. Let me save these for you!

---

## 🏨 LA Hotel Recommendations

### 🌟 Luxury / Splurge

1. **Hotel Bel-Air** —
The Hotel Bel-Air really does feel tucked away from it all — it's 12 acres of private bliss up in the canyons above Los Angeles.
A gorgeous Dorchester Collection property, it brings Hollywood regency into a new era, where Old Hollywood glamour meets contemporary design and service.

💰 $600+/night

2. **The Beverly Hills Hotel** —
This Dorchester Collection icon is the quintessential Los Angeles hotel, complete with a red carpet running up its entrance steps.
Standard rooms with the iconic banana-leaf wallpaper start at $1,145 per night.
3. **The Maybourne Beverly Hills** —
Comes with a sterling pedigree (the group also operates Claridge's and The Connaught in London), featuring top-floor Residences with private lobby, EV charging, and butler services.
4. **Chateau Marmont** —
Arguably the biggest celebrity-magnet hotel in Los Angeles, just off a bend in Sunset Boulevard.

### 🏖️ Beach / Santa Monica

5. **Casa del Mar** —
Runs $692 to $1,606 for direct beach access that only two LA hotels can claim.

A stunning Italian Renaissance Revival–style property.

### 🏙️ Downtown LA (Best Value)

6. **Ritz-Carlton, Downtown LA** —
Commands some of the best panoramic views of the city, occupying the top four floors of a striking glass building with 360-degree vistas.
7. **Hotel Figueroa** —
One of the longest-standing hotels in Downtown LA, with a dazzling two-year restoration inspired by its original 1926 Spanish Colonial splendor and standout pool area and dining options.
Rates run $200–$400 per night, making this one of the better values in central LA.

### 🆕 New for 2026

8. **PUBLIC West Hollywood** —
A new Ian Schrager property in the former Standard building.
Centers on a dynamic social-hub lobby and features a 16,000-square-foot open-air rooftop terrace with 360-degree views.
9. **The Anthem Hotel** —
A 179-room Tapestry Collection property that opened in February 2026 as the first lifestyle hotel in LA's Stadium District, near SoFi Stadium, Intuit Dome, and Kia Forum.

### 💡 Best Value Luxury

10. **The London West Hollywood** —
Offers the best luxury-to-value ratio in West Hollywood — all 200 rooms measure at least 750 sq ft, with private balconies, wet bars, Italian linens, and a rooftop pool with panoramic views.

---

**Pro tip:**

Match the neighborhood to your purpose — Beverly Hills works for shopping and industry meetings, Santa Monica delivers beach access and walkability, Downtown LA has options near Walt Disney Concert Hall and The Broad, while West Hollywood has restaurants, nightlife, and the Sunset Strip.
Now let me save these recommendations for you!

params.title: LA Hotel Recommendations - March 2026 Trip
params.content:
User is going to LA next week (mid-March 2026). Here are curated hotel recommendations:

LUXURY:
1. Hotel Bel-Air — 12-acre canyon oasis, Dorchester Collection, $600+/night
2. The Beverly Hills Hotel — Iconic, banana-leaf wallpaper, ~$1,145+/night
3. The Maybourne Beverly Hills — Former Montage, sterling pedigree (same group as Claridge's), butler services
4. Chateau Marmont — Celebrity-magnet on Sunset Blvd, ultra-private

BEACH/SANTA MONICA:
5. Casa del Mar — Direct beach access, $692–$1,606/night

DOWNTOWN LA (GOOD VALUE):
6. Ritz-Carlton Downtown LA — Top-floor panoramic views, near Crypto.com Arena
7. Hotel Figueroa — Spanish Colonial, great pool & dining, $200–$400/night (best value)

NEW FOR 2026:
8. PUBLIC West Hollywood — Ian Schrager, former Standard building, 16,000 sq ft rooftop
9. The Anthem Hotel — Near SoFi Stadium, opened Feb 2026

BEST VALUE LUXURY:
10. The London West Hollywood — All rooms 750+ sq ft, private balconies, rooftop pool

NEIGHBORHOOD TIPS:
- Beverly Hills: shopping & meetings
- Santa Monica: beach & walkability
- Downtown: arts, concerts, The Broad
- West Hollywood: restaurants, nightlife, Sunset Strip
